name: library-detection
description: Detect project stack from package manifests (package.json, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pubspec.yaml, CMakeLists.txt). Auto-identify frameworks, test tools, and build systems for onboarding.
capabilities: Scans package manifests across 12+ file types including package.json, pyproject.toml, go.mod, and Cargo.toml
Identifies frameworks from dependency patterns such as React, FastAPI, Django, and Express
Detects test frameworks and build tools including pytest, vitest, Jest, Vite, and webpack
Classifies dependencies into production, development, peer, and optional categories
Outputs structured analysis in JSON, markdown, or toon format
Recognizes databases and cloud providers from SDK package patterns
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: All 190 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files (SKILL.md, cookbook/manifest-parsing.md) with instructional content. The flagged 'external_commands' are example shell snippets in code blocks, not executable code. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings refer to JSON Schema URL references, not crypto operations. The 'obfuscation' heuristic misinterpreted documentation examples as suspicious code. No executable code, network calls, file writes, or system access capabilities exist.
valueStatement: This skill identifies the technology stack of any project by scanning package manifests and configuration files. It helps developers quickly understand project dependencies, frameworks, and build tools without manual investigation.
userTitle: Detect project technology stacks from manifests
limitations: Only analyzes manifest files, not actual code imports or runtime dependencies
Cannot detect implicit dependencies or dynamic imports used in code
Requires manifests to be present in standard project locations to function

useCases.title: New team member onboarding
useCases.description: Quickly understand project tech stack when joining a new codebase

useCases.title: Automated documentation
useCases.description: Generate stack documentation and identify relevant doc sources automatically

useCases.title: Environment setup
useCases.description: Identify required tools, test frameworks, and build systems for CI/CD configuration

promptTemplates.title: Basic stack scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Use library-detection to identify the technology stack of this project. List the languages, frameworks, test tools, and build systems found.

promptTemplates.title: Framework verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Run library-detection to confirm the frameworks and libraries used in this project. Check both production and development dependencies.

promptTemplates.title: Test framework discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Use library-detection to identify all test frameworks and testing utilities in this project. Include version information where available.

promptTemplates.title: Full stack report
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute library-detection with full detail. Output the complete stack analysis including languages, frameworks, databases, cloud providers, and CI/CD tools.

bestPractices: Always verify detected frameworks against actual imports when precision is critical
Use the OUTPUT_FORMAT variable to match downstream processing requirements
Cross-reference requirements.txt with pyproject.toml for Python projects
antiPatterns: Assuming framework usage without checking actual imports or configuration
Skipping manifest analysis for supposedly simple projects
Reporting libraries without verifying they are actually used in the codebase

faq.question: Which package manifest formats are supported?
faq.answer: Supports package.json, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pubspec.yaml, CMakeLists.txt, meson.build, pom.xml, build.gradle, Gemfile, composer.json.

faq.question: What is the maximum scan depth?
faq.answer: Default depth is 3 directories. Configure with SCAN_DEPTH variable. Deeper scans may impact performance on large monorepos.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Yes. Other skills can reference library-detection output for documentation discovery, tool configuration, and agent routing based on detected stack.

faq.question: Is project data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads local manifest files and outputs analysis. No network calls are made and no data leaves the local environment.

faq.question: Why did detection miss some dependencies?
faq.answer: Ensure manifests exist in standard locations. Some dependencies may be dynamically imported or defined in lock files not directly parsed.

faq.question: How does this compare to dependency scanning tools?
faq.answer: This skill analyzes manifest structure rather than lock files. It provides faster broad-stroke identification suitable for onboarding and documentation.
